title: power_outage
description: A facility-wide power outage interrupts critical systems.

timeline.title: Power Loss
timeline.description: Main power fails across multiple floors. Emergency lighting activated automatically.

timeline.title: Emergency Lighting Engaged
timeline.description: Battery-powered emergency lights operational. Account for all personnel in affected areas.

timeline.title: Generator Activation
timeline.description: Backup generator engaged but capacity is limited. Prioritize critical systems only.

timeline.title: Load Prioritization
timeline.description: Identify and power essential life safety systems, communications, and security equipment.

timeline.title: System Stabilization
timeline.description: Generator running stable. Monitor fuel levels and coordinate with utility company on restoration ETA.

timeline.title: Grid Power Restoration
timeline.description: Utility power restored. Gradually transfer load back to main grid and secure generator.

node-1.title: Sudden Power Failure
node-1.situation: All main power has failed across multiple floors. Emergency lighting has activated automatically. Staff are concerned and some areas are completely dark.
node-1.question: What is your immediate priority?

options.label: A
options.text: Confirm emergency lighting is active throughout the facility and account for all personnel.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt to restore main power immediately by resetting the main breaker.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait for staff to report any issues before taking action.

node-2.title: Activating Backup Generator
node-2.situation: Emergency lighting is confirmed operational. The backup generator is available but has limited capacity and cannot support all systems simultaneously.
node-2.question: How do you manage the generator activation?

options.label: A
options.text: Activate the generator and prioritize life safety systems, communications, and security equipment only.

options.label: B
options.text: Connect all systems to the generator to restore normal operations as quickly as possible.

node-3.title: Critical Systems Running
node-3.situation: The generator is running and critical systems are online. However, fuel levels need monitoring and the utility company has not yet provided a restoration ETA.
node-3.question: What steps do you take to maintain stability?

options.label: A
options.text: Assign someone to monitor fuel levels and contact the utility company for a restoration update.

options.label: B
options.text: Begin restoring non-critical systems to the generator to improve staff comfort.

node-4.title: Grid Power Restored
node-4.situation: The utility company has confirmed grid power will be restored within minutes. The generator needs to be carefully transferred back to avoid equipment damage.
node-4.question: How do you manage the power restoration?

options.label: A
options.text: Gradually transfer load back to the main grid and then safely shut down and secure the generator.

options.label: B
options.text: Switch all systems back to grid power at once to restore operations as fast as possible.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Grid power has been fully restored, the generator has been shut down and secured, and all critical systems are back online with no data loss.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
